changes_summary: Addressed all three significant flags without scope growth. Key revisions: (1) Step 6 now stops pointer events (`onPointerDown` + `onPointerUp` + `onClick`) on the home button, not just click — fixes FLAG-001 since `ToolCard` navigates on `onPointerUp`. (2) Step 4 explicitly removes the planned 'repair stale id by calling update()' write, because `useUserUIState.update` is 200 ms debounced with unmount-clear and would drop writes issued from a redirect-then-navigate component — fixes FLAG-002. The redirect now falls back at runtime without rewriting storage; stale ids are rare and gated-off ids must be preserved. (3) Added a new Step 3 that extracts `isToolEligible` into `src/shared/lib/tooling/toolEligibility.ts`, mirroring the env + cloud-mode logic in `ToolsPane.isToolVisible`, and Step 4 uses it so the redirect honors the same gating the sidebar uses — fixes FLAG-003. Renumbered downstream steps. Updated success criteria: removed the now-incorrect 'overwrites stale id' criterion and replaced with 'falls back without overwriting'; added a criterion for the pointer-event handling. Promoted the gated-tool fallback criterion from `info` to `must` since FLAG-003 made it a real correctness gate. The eligibility helper extraction is justified by FLAG-003, not scope creep — the redirect literally cannot satisfy its requirement without it.
questions: Should the home button appear on EVERY tool card (including the currently-default one, just rendered highlighted), or should it be hidden on the default and only shown on hover for non-defaults? Assuming always-visible per the brief's 'highlighted for the current default' language.

If the user has set a default tool that later becomes invisible (env or cloud-mode gated), should the redirect (a) silently fall back without touching the stored preference, (b) fall back AND reset, or (c) navigate there anyway? Assuming (a) — preserves the preference for when the gate re-opens. The revised plan now actively requires (a) because the debounced-write constraint makes (b) unreliable from the redirect path.
Should 'assistant' tools also be eligible to be set as default? Assuming yes — universal home button matches the brief's 'home button on each tool entry' phrasing and is simpler.

success_criteria.criterion: `tsc --noEmit` (or the project's typecheck) passes with the new `defaultTool` key added to `UISettings` and `decodeDefaultTool` wired into `normalizeUserUISetting`.
`src/shared/lib/tooling/toolManifest.ts` sets `paneSection: 'main'` for the `videoEditorSettings` entry, and the tool appears under the 'Main Tools' section of `ToolsPane` at runtime.

The home button's pointer event handlers (`onPointerDown` + `onPointerUp` + `onClick`) all call `e.stopPropagation()`, so pressing the home icon does NOT trigger the parent ToolCard's `onPointerUp` navigation.
Clicking the home button on a tool card calls `updateDefaultTool({ toolId })` and updates the highlighted state; the user remains on the current tool's route.
After setting a non-default tool as default and reloading the app at `/`, `/tools`, or post-login, the user lands on the tool whose id matches `users.settings.ui.defaultTool.toolId`.
The currently-default tool card renders the home icon in a visually distinct (filled/accented) state; all other cards render the home icon in a muted/outline state.
Users with no stored `defaultTool` preference still land on `travel-between-images` (backwards-compatible default).
If the stored `toolId` references a tool missing from `toolRuntimeManifest`, the redirect falls back to `travel-between-images` at runtime and does NOT rewrite the stored preference.

If the stored `toolId` references a tool that is currently hidden by env or cloud-mode gate (per `isToolEligible`), the redirect falls back to `travel-between-images` and does NOT rewrite the stored preference, so it re-activates when the gate re-opens.
`isToolEligible` is extracted to a shared module and used by both `ToolsPane.isToolVisible` and `DefaultToolRedirect`, so sidebar visibility and redirect eligibility cannot drift.
No new Supabase migration file is added (the change reuses the existing JSONB `users.settings.ui` column).
Home button has `aria-label`, `type="button"`, and a tooltip; it is keyboard-focusable.
No other call sites hardcoding `/tools/travel-between-images` remain (outside of the `DefaultToolRedirect` fallback).
Manual smoke test: home button persists across page reloads and across browser sessions for the same user.

Manual smoke test: setting default to character-animate, then toggling cloud generation off and reloading, lands on travel-between-images without crashing AND the stored preference is preserved (re-enabling cloud restores the character-animate landing).

assumptions: The home button is rendered on every ToolCard (main + assistant sections), not just main tools.
Fallback tool id is 'travel-between-images' (preserves today's behavior).

Stale stored toolId (tool removed from manifest): redirect falls back at runtime to 'travel-between-images' but does NOT rewrite the stored value. Same behavior for env/cloud-gated tools. Rationale: `useUserUIState.update` is 200 ms debounced and cleared on unmount, so a repair write from a redirect-then-navigate component would be dropped; and gated tools must keep the preference for when the gate re-opens.
No new Supabase migration is needed — `users.settings` is JSONB and `useUserUIState` handles backfill on first read.
The loader-to-component conversion for `/tools` is acceptable; a brief `<ReighLoading />` flash during the Supabase fetch matches existing lazy-load UX.
No analytics/telemetry event needs to fire when the user changes their default tool.
Home-button icon uses lucide's `Home`, sized w-4 h-4, with `fill-current` when active and muted foreground otherwise. No new design token needed.
The eligibility helper `isToolEligible` is a pure function; the existing `isToolVisible` in `ToolsPane.tsx` becomes a thin wrapper. No behavior change for the sidebar from this refactor.
